zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Radcliffe於1305年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾拉德克利夫時，他的父親William de Radcliffe37歲，母親Margaret De Hindley35歲。他於1323年7月19日與Maud Matilda de Leigh在英國英格蘭藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他在英國藍卡爾被列為high sheriff of lancashire。他去世於1380年7月19日在英國藍卡爾洛桑達爾享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭藍卡爾巴貝。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Radcliffe于1305年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾拉德克利夫时，他的父亲William de Radcliffe37岁，母亲Margaret De Hindley35岁。他于1323年7月19日与Maud Matilda de Leigh在英国英格兰藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他在英国藍卡爾被列为high sheriff of lancashire。他去世于1380年7月19日在英国藍卡爾洛桑達爾享年75岁，埋葬在英国英格兰藍卡爾巴貝。

nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Radcliffe was born in 1305, in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Radcliffe, was 37 and his mother, Margaret De Hindley, was 35. He married Maud Matilda de Leigh on 19 July 1323, in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. His occupation is listed as high sheriff of lancashire in Lancashire, England. He died on 19 July 1380, in Rossendale, Lancashire, England, at the age of 75, and was buried in Bacup, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Radcliffe est né en 1305, à Radcliffe, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Radcliffe, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Margaret De Hindley, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Maud Matilda de Leigh le 19 juillet 1323, à Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of lancashire à Lancashire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1380, à Rossendale, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Bacup, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Radcliffe 1305 in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Radcliffe 37 und seine Mutter Margaret De Hindley 35. Er hat am 19. Juli 1323 in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Maud Matilda de Leigh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. sein Beruf ist als high sheriff of lancashire in Lancashire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 19. Juli 1380 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Rossendale, Lancashire, England und wurde in Bacup, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Radcliffe è nato il nel 1305 nel Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Radcliffe, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Margaret De Hindley, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Maud Matilda de Leigh il 19 luglio 1323, nel Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. La sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of lancashire nel Lancashire, England. Morì il 19 luglio 1380, nel Rossendale, Lancashire, England, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolto a Bacup, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Radcliffeが1305年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアラドクリフで生まれた時，父William de Radcliffeは37歳，母Margaret De Hindleyは35歳であった。1323年7月19日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアトラフォード マノーでMaud Matilda de Leighと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。イギリスランカシュアイアでの職業はhigh sheriff of lancashireと記録されている。彼は1380年7月19日にイギリスランカシュアイアロセンダルで75歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアバクウップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Radcliffe(이)가 1305년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 라드클리프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Radcliffe(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret De Hindley(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1323년 7월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 트래퍼드 먼어에서 Maud Matilda de Leigh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 랭카실르에서 high sheriff of lancashire이었다. 그는 1380년 7월 19일 영국 랭카실르 로센데일에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 바c업에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Radcliffe nasceu em 1305, em Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Radcliffe, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Margaret De Hindley, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud Matilda de Leigh em 19 de julho de 1323, em Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of lancashire em Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 19 de julho de 1380, em Rossendale, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Bacup, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Radcliffe родился в 1305 г. в местности радклифф, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Radcliffe было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret De Hindley, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Matilda de Leigh 19 июля 1323 г. в местности траффорд манор, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.  Он работал по профессии high sheriff of lancashire в местности ланкашир, Англия. Он умер 19 июля 1380 г. в местности россендеил, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности бакуп, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Radcliffe nació en 1305, en Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Radcliffe, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Margaret De Hindley, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Maud Matilda de Leigh el 19 de julio de 1323, en Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Su ocupación registrada era high sheriff of lancashire en Lancashire, Inglaterra. Murió el 19 de julio de 1380, en Rossendale, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Bacup, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
